content.full_text:
July 8, 1977

A-77-07-030

Mr. John P. Falconer
Secretary/Treasurer
San Diego County Deputy
Sheriffs' Association
5555 Mildred Street
San Diego, California 92110

Dear Mr. Falconer:

Thank you for your letter of June 29, 1977. In your letter
you ask if an association can make political contributions
with funds that have not been specifically set aside for that
purpose. There is nothing in the Political Reform Act that
prohibits associations from making political contributions
from existing funds. The Association's funds need not be
"earmarked" (set aside) for political purposes in order to
make a political contribution.

You also ask whether an association must file as a committee if
the donations are under $500 total for the calendar year. If the
Association's political contributions do not total $500, there
is no obligation to file as a committee. However, if within the
same calendar year the Association begins collecting money for
the purpose of making a political contribution, that total must
be added to the contributions already made. When the cumulative
amount totals $500 the Association becomes a committee and must
file accordingly.

I hope the above clarifies for you the questions posed in your
letter. Please let me know if I can be of further assistance.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Arnold L. Markey                     -2-                    August 3, 1977


Enclosed are some guidelines and forms which should be of some
assistance to you. If you require additional information, please
do not hesitate to let us know or contact your local election
office in the city or county clerk's office.

Very truly yours,



Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
 Coordinator

RSK/st

Enclosures

170230

San Diego County Deputy Sheriffs' Association

5555 MILDRED STREET
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92110
TELEPHONE 291-2570

June 29, 1977

Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Calif. 95804

Dear Ms. Kvasager:

Per our telephone conversation date same, I am forwarding
the following questions for clarification.

1. Can an association make political contributions with
funds that have not been specifically set aside for
that purpose?

2. Must an association file as a committee if the donations
are under $500.00 total for the year?

I would appreciate an expedient answer, at your convenience
of course. Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,

John F. Falconer, Sec/Treasurer
S.D.C.D.S.A.

JFF/pja

cc: File

sections.question:
1. Can an association make political contributions with funds that have not been specifically set aside for that purpose?

2. Must an association file as a committee if the donations are under $500.00 total for the year?
sections.conclusion: There is nothing in the Political Reform Act that prohibits associations from making political contributions from existing funds. The Association's funds need not be "earmarked" (set aside) for political purposes in order to make a political contribution. If the Association's political contributions do not total $500, there is no obligation to file as a committee. However, if within the same calendar year the Association begins collecting money for the purpose of making a political contribution, that total must be added to the contributions already made. When the cumulative amount totals $500 the Association becomes a committee and must file accordingly.
sections.question_synthetic: The San Diego County Deputy Sheriffs' Association asks whether associations can make political contributions from existing funds without earmarking them for that purpose, and whether filing as a committee is required if total donations are below $500 in a calendar year.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Associations may make political contributions from existing funds without earmarking them, and no committee filing is required if total contributions remain below $500 in a calendar year. However, when contributions reach $500 cumulatively—including any newly collected funds designated for political purposes—the association must register and file as a committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (June 29, 1977) and FPPC response letters (July 8 and August 3, 1977). Primary response by Mary Ann Kvasager provides clear Q&A format. Follow-up letter by Regina Siciliano-Kutchins is administrative in nature.

QUESTION: 1. Can an association make political contributions with funds that have not been specifically set aside for that purpose?

2. Must an association file as a committee if the donations are under $500.00 total for the year?

CONCLUSION: There is nothing in the Political Reform Act that prohibits associations from making political contributions from existing funds. The Association's funds need not be "earmarked" (set aside) for political purposes in order to make a political contribution. If the Association's political contributions do not total $500, there is no obligation to file as a committee. However, if within the same calendar year the Association begins collecting money for the purpose of making a political contribution, that total must be added to the contributions already made. When the cumulative amount totals $500 the Association becomes a committee and must file accordingly.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the San Diego County Deputy Sheriffs' Association that political contributions can be made from general funds without earmarking, and filing as a committee is only required when cumulative contributions reach $500 in a calendar year.
